mentions.note: Retail and tech play; diversifies portfolio across sectors.
Dual retail and tech exposure; part of diversified portfolio; sector rotation beneficiary.
Worst MAG7 performer last year but still up slightly YTD; more defensive positioning; holding up better than peers.
Worst performing mega-cap last year but slightly up YTD; holding up better than peers in quality tech selloff.
Hyperscaler benefiting from AI data center orders; exposed to OpenAI's ability to fund compute commitments.
Historic Cramer Fang+ holding; facing temporary money outflows to storage plays but Cramer believes capital will return to Magnificent 7 as storage rally peaks.
Rallied on news of big planned layoffs post-Intel; money flowing back to Mag 7 from shortage plays.
Developing own chips but massive Nvidia customer. Hyperscaler demand from Amazon enormous and growing — validates Nvidia ecosystem strength.
Controversial stock; formerly roared on earnings but now gets faded on strength and sells off on weakness; owns for Charitable Trust but has not been good returner; let earnings speak.

17% revenue growth, 75% EPS growth, AWS +28% (best in 4 years), custom chip business at $20B run rate, operating income +30%; guidance mixed on operating income; parabolic move consolidating, CEO Jassy best explainer of data center ROI; stock should go higher but move overstretched.

Andy Jassy noted in shareholder letter that Amazon actually lowered prices vs. last year; valuable FedEx customer (recent relationship); competing 3PL offering is non-asset play, not threat to FedEx true end-to-end network; win-win relationship.
Magnificent Seven member owned by Cramer's charitable trust. Original FANG stock; demonstrated ability to scale despite heavy borrowing; e-commerce and cloud dominance; secular megatrend.
Important AI capabilities; mentioned as context for tech dominance but not specifically recommended for action.
Cramer is 'huge believer'—Prime, AWS, advertising all thriving. Own AI chips hold value equal to Nvidia's at lower cost; likely reason Nvidia stalled while Amazon surged.
Has own chips; pointedly absent from Jensen's winners list but Cramer likes the trio; Berkshire Hathaway buying $10B in equity raise signals confidence; deep enterprise relationship.
Heavy data center spending may necessitate equity raising similar to Microsoft; monitor for secondary offering announcements that would pressure stock and shareholders.
May do a fundraiser per Cramer's speculation. Supply could hit stock if they tap capital markets.
Developing Trainium and Trainium alternatives to reduce Nvidia chip reliance. If equity offering follows Google's, will dilute supply further just before SpaceX IPO.
Down 12% as Wall Street ignores strong advertising/Prime momentum and demands free cash flow proof from AI spending next year or the stock gets punished further.
'Mistaken identity' stock unfairly punished in 2025 despite strong AWS reacceleration, retail, and advertising growth.
AWS strong but $200B capex shock overshadowed; requires faith in Jassy's execution; own for Travel Trust but hard sell.
Being beaten by Walmart in web services, by Google in other areas; doesn't get respect it deserves; market has reached unfavorable verdict despite quality.
Mentioned in Cloudflare CEO's small-business consolidation risk (AI agents favor big e-commerce); no specific price action or recommendation.
Broke negative range today (+4%, to $226), rallying $8 after weeks of pressure; AWS unlikely to be wiped out by Anthropic; sentiment inflection signal.
Data center critical customer; okay showing today but rally needed more momentum.
Negative whispers consumer slowdown will hurt Prime; Cramer knows Amazon tight as drum, doesn't leak; no real evidence; hold through noise.
Andy Jassy's letter proves $200B 2026 AI capex backed by customer commitments; AWS $161B revenue, custom chips 'on fire', satellite/drone logistics transformative; own and buy on dips.
Jennifer's portfolio; retail but also lot of tech; amazing conglomerate; keep in portfolio for tech/retail blend.
Rebounded after Jassy's strategic letter clarified AWS slowdown narrative; satellite acquisition added momentum. Weakness on retail, slowing AWS, and CEO comparison to Bezos fears unwarranted.
AWS cloud infrastructure; big contract with Anthropic announced; third-layer AI infrastructure provider.
Stock soaring since Andy Jassy's vision letter; own for charitable trust, can't buy more due to size; pullback may be buying opportunity.
Mega-cap tech earnings tomorrow (earnings D-Day); key risk if big capex without matching revenue growth. Held in trust.
AWS grew 28% (insanely strong); Andy Jassy's division proving doubters wrong; may need more power/compute for data center work; fantastic cloud business.
Ultimate trade-down play in rising-rate environment; hyperscaler with AWS, new supply-chain services, satellite broadband, and AI capex positioning it as last man standing. Own it.

Supply-chain services launching (3M, P&G, Lands' End, American Eagle clients) unlocks trillions for SMBs; AWS AI business at $15B run rate (260x faster than early AWS); Trainium/Graviton chips at $20B+ (or $50B if sold as products); Leo satellite broadband solves digital divide; healthcare (One Medical, pharmacy) margin accretive; live sports and Prime Video driving engagement. All-in bet on AI as biggest technology transformation; capex pays off in 5-30 years with massive ROIC/margin upside.
Data center capex of tens of billions now securing decades of colossal profits; only two clear public winners in infrastructure race.
AWS building out massive AI data center capacity; competing with Google and others.
